vulnerability.name: Cron Tar Wildcard Privilege Escalation
vulnerability.author: Rosie Fletcher
vulnerability.description:
A cron job runs tar with a wildcard (*) inside a user-writable directory. This can be exploited using tar checkpoint flag injection by creating specially-named files (--checkpoint=1 and --checkpoint-action=exec=sh exploit.sh) to execute arbitrary commands as the cron user.
By default, the backup directory is world-writable (mode 0777) allowing any user to exploit. Optionally, write access can be restricted to a specific user via the restrict_write_user parameter, enabling targeted privilege escalation scenarios where only that user can exploit the vulnerability. When restrict_write_user is set, crontab viewing via sudo is also restricted to that user only.

CyBOK.keyword: Vulnerabilities and attacks on access control misconfigurations
